title: Local model: finite 2-torsion group acts freely & cocompactly on a finite-dim Q-acyclic manifold

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: To realize $\Gamma$ (which has 2-torsion) as $\pi_1$ of a closed manifold with $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic universal cover, one needs, for each finite subgroup $F\le\Gamma$ (at minimum $\mathbb{Z}/2$), a **free, smooth, cocompact action on a finite-dimensional $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic manifold** $W_F$. The rational triviality $H^{>0}(F;\mathbb{Q})=0$ (transfer/averaging) is the heuristic that removes the rational obstruction, but constructing an actual finite-dimensional free **manifold** model — with controlled dimension/parity and the correct local structure to be glued into the global construction — is a genuine obligation. By Smith, $W_F$ must have nontrivial mod-2 homology. Prove existence of $W_F$.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolution of q7-4 (local free Q-acyclic models).** Rewrote solution.tex to construct and prove correct the local building block.

**Key correction first.** The literal demand — a *free cocompact action on a Q-acyclic manifold* — is **impossible** for |F|>1. If F acts freely on a finite complex W then χ(W)=|F|·χ(W/F), so |F| | χ(W); but Q-acyclic forces χ(W)=1, contradiction (Lemma: Euler-characteristic obstruction). This holds for compact W with or without boundary. So the naive object cannot exist.

**Correct local object.** A free F-manifold W_F whose *quotient* V_F = W_F/F is Q-acyclic, with ∂V_F = the link L = S(V)/F of the stratum (V = normal slice, V^F=0). Then χ(V_F)=1, χ(W_F)=|F| — consistent. Crucially W_F itself is NOT Q-acyclic (χ=|F|≠1) and carries nonzero mod-2 homology, exactly as Smith theory demands; no conflict.

**What is proved.**
1. **Reduction (Lemma):** an atomic local model exists iff the link L bounds a compact smooth Q-acyclic manifold with the right boundary monodromy to F; W_F is then the F-cover.
2. **Atomic existence theorem (full surgery proof):** every rational homology sphere L^{m-1} (m-1≥5) that is null-bordant in Ω^SO bounds a compact smooth Q-acyclic V_F. Proof: start with any null-bordism; surger to connected + simply-connected; kill rational homology below the middle (Hurewicz + general position, normal bundles trivial in range); in the middle dimension use that L being a QHS makes the rational intersection form nonsingular — for odd middle it is hyperbolic hence metabolic, for even middle (d=4j) adjust the signature to 0 by interior connected-sum with ∓CP^{2j} (Novikov additivity, sign(CP^{2j})=1) and then surger a rational Lagrangian. No odd-dimensional obstruction; the Arf/torsion residue is invisible over Q.
3. **Minimal case F=Z/2 — UNCONDITIONAL (Corollary):** the link is RP^{m-1}; for m even it is an oriented QHS that bounds explicitly the oriented disk bundle D(O(2)) over CP^{(m-2)/2} (its circle bundle of Euler class 2h is S^{m-1}/{±1}=RP^{m-1}), so [L]=0 in Ω^SO; surgery gives Q-acyclic V_{Z/2} with ∂=RP^{m-1}, and W_{Z/2} (∂=S^{m-1}, antipodal) with χ=2 (so explicitly not Z/2-acyclic). Slice dimension/parity constraints are met by stabilizing V by antipodal R^2's.
4. **General finite 2-groups (Proposition):** when F does not act freely on S(V), the link is itself an orbifold; build V_F by induction over isotropy strata, resolving deeper strata by the models W_{F'} (F'⊊F) and surgering the resolved (manifold) link. 

**Honest scope on general 2-groups.** The one nonformal input at each inductive stage is *integral* null-bordance of the resolved link in Ω^SO. This (a) holds on the nose for Z/2 and whenever the link bounds; (b) holds **rationally always**: the reduced free bordism class [S(V)→BF] is torsion since Ω̃^SO_*(BF)⊗Q ≅ H̃_*(BF;Q)⊗(Ω^SO_*⊗Q)=0 (as H^{>0}(F;Q)=0), so N copies bound. The downstream resolution (q7-5, q7-7) only consumes the *rational* homology equivalence (16)/eq:RFNF, which the N-fold or surgered model supplies. I did not eliminate the 2-primary integral bordism obstruction for general 2-groups beyond the rational statement; it is explicitly flagged.

**Status:** the minimal/required case (Z/2) is fully and unconditionally resolved; the general finite-2-group case is reduced to a clearly-stated, rationally-discharged bordism input. Marking in_progress because the integral 2-group obstruction is handled only rationally (which is, however, exactly what the global construction needs).

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (critic-agent)

# Critic Analysis — Problem q7 (alias `geomtopology_rm14k`)

**Problem (Shmuel Weinberger).** Let $\Gamma$ be a uniform (cocompact) lattice in a
real semisimple Lie group $G$, and suppose $\Gamma$ contains some $2$-torsion. Is it
possible for $\Gamma$ to be the fundamental group of a *closed* manifold (compact,
without boundary) whose universal cover is *acyclic over $\mathbb{Q}$*?

This is an existence/possibility question. A complete solution must (i) decide the
answer (Yes/No), and (ii) either construct such a manifold (if Yes) or prove an
obstruction ruling it out (if No). The literature attribution (Weinberger) and the
in-progress solver work both point to the answer being **Yes — such a manifold can be
constructed** — and the difficulty lies entirely in the *construction*. The analysis
below is organized around what such a construction must establish.

---

## Background

**Key objects.**
- $G$ real semisimple Lie group, $K\le G$ maximal compact, $X=G/K$ the associated
  symmetric space: contractible, $\dim X = n$, with a proper smooth $G$-action.
- $\Gamma\le G$ uniform lattice: acts properly discontinuously and cocompactly on $X$.
  If $G$ is noncompact, $\Gamma$ is infinite.
- $\Gamma$ has $2$-torsion: there is a subgroup $F\cong\mathbb{Z}/2$ (and possibly larger
  finite subgroups). Finitely many conjugacy classes of finite subgroups (cocompactness).

**Classical baseline (why naive aspherical models fail).**
If $\widetilde M$ were *contractible* (i.e. $M$ aspherical, a $K(\Gamma,1)$-manifold),
then $\Gamma=\pi_1(M)$ would act freely on the finite-dimensional contractible space
$\widetilde M$. But by **Smith theory**, $\mathbb{Z}/p$ cannot act freely on a
finite-dimensional $\mathbb{F}_p$-acyclic space; hence $\Gamma$ would be torsion-free.
Since $\Gamma$ has $2$-torsion, no closed *aspherical* model exists. The whole point of
relaxing "contractible" to "$\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic universal cover" is precisely to evade
this obstruction.

**Why $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic is the right relaxation.**
Smith theory is a *mod-$p$* phenomenon. A finite-dimensional $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic space
may carry substantial mod-$2$ homology, and $\mathbb{Z}/2$ *can* act freely on
$\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic finite complexes (e.g. building blocks modeled on free actions whose
quotients are $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic). So the mod-$p$ Smith obstruction does **not** apply,
which is exactly why the answer can be Yes.

**Tools likely needed.**
1. *Transfer / averaging:* For finite $F$ acting on a space $Y$,
   $H^{>0}(F;\mathbb{Q})=0$ and the transfer argument gives
   $H^*(Y/F;\mathbb{Q})\cong H^*(Y;\mathbb{Q})^F$; rationally, free $F$-quotients of
   $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic spaces are $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic.
2. *Smith theory / Tate cohomology:* mod-$2$ constraints; periodicity for $\mathbb{Z}/2$.
3. *Euler characteristic obstruction:* for a free $F$-action on a finite complex $W$,
   $\chi(W)=|F|\,\chi(W/F)$, so $|F|\mid\chi(W)$.
4. *Poincaré duality / surgery theory:* to upgrade a homotopy-theoretic model to a smooth
   (or topological) *closed manifold* with the prescribed $\pi_1$ and $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic
   cover. Wall finiteness and surgery realization of Poincaré complexes.
5. *Bredon homology / equivariant gluing:* to assemble local free $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic
   models over the orbit structure of $\Gamma\curvearrowright X$.

---

## Proof Structure

A complete (constructive) proof should proceed in stages:

**Stage A — Reduce to a finite-group local model.**
Identify the finite subgroups $F\le\Gamma$ (at least $\mathbb{Z}/2$). The orbifold
$X/\Gamma$ has singular strata indexed by conjugacy classes of finite subgroups. Near a
fixed point of $F$, the local model is the linear $F$-action on a slice
$V\cong\mathbb{R}^k$. One must replace the singular orbifold point by a *free*
$\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic manifold piece.

**Stage B — Construct the local free $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic building block $W_F$.**
For each finite $F$, construct a compact manifold $W_F$ with free $F$-action such that:
- the quotient $V_F=W_F/F$ is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic (rel its boundary, appropriately),
- $\partial W_F$ matches the link of the singular stratum (i.e. equivariantly identifies
  with the unit sphere $S(V)$ of the slice representation, so $\partial W_F=S(V)$ with the
  given linear $F$-action), allowing it to be glued in place of the singular cone.

**IMPORTANT CAVEAT (Euler characteristic).** A *closed* (or compact) manifold on which
$F$ acts freely and whose total space is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic is **impossible** for
$|F|>1$: $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic $\Rightarrow \chi(W)=1$, but freeness $\Rightarrow
|F|\mid\chi(W)$. So the correct local object is a manifold $W_F$ *with boundary* whose
*quotient* $V_F=W_F/F$ is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic, NOT a closed $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic free
$F$-manifold. This distinction is essential and any proof must respect it.

**Stage C — Global assembly.**
Excise equivariant cone neighborhoods of the singular strata from $X$ and glue in the
$W_F$ pieces $\Gamma$-equivariantly (over orbits), producing a free, proper, cocompact
$\Gamma$-manifold $\widetilde M$. Take $M=\widetilde M/\Gamma$.

**Stage D — Verify the two required properties.**
1. $\pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$: the gluing must not change $\pi_1$ (van Kampen; control
   $\pi_1$ of the boundary spheres/links and of the inserted pieces, e.g. high
   connectivity of $W_F$ below the middle dimension).
2. $\widetilde M$ is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic: a Mayer–Vietoris / spectral sequence
   computation showing the $\mathbb{Q}$-homology of the modified $X$ collapses to that of
   a point, using that each inserted piece is $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic and the links are
   $\mathbb{Q}$-homology spheres.
3. $M$ is a *closed* manifold without boundary: the boundaries introduced in Stage B must
   cancel against the excised links so the assembled $\widetilde M$ (hence $M$) is
   boundaryless. Dimension bookkeeping and matching boundary identifications are required.

---

## Gap Analysis

(No `solution.tex` is present in the critic workspace; gaps are stated against the
*proof obligations* a solver must discharge. The existing tracker issue q7-4 already
isolates the central local-model gap; the gaps below extend coverage to the global
construction and verification, which a complete proof additionally requires.)

- **G1 (Local model existence — already tracked as q7-4).** Construct, for each finite
  $F\le\Gamma$, a compact free $F$-manifold $W_F$ with $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic quotient and
  prescribed boundary $\partial W_F=S(V)$ (slice sphere). The Euler-characteristic
  obstruction forbids a closed $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic free model, so the boundary version
  must be built and its $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity (rel structure) proved.

- **G2 (Boundary/link matching).** Prove that $\partial W_F$ can be taken
  $F$-equivariantly diffeomorphic to the unit sphere $S(V)$ of the actual slice
  representation $V$ occurring in $X$ at the $F$-fixed stratum, so the gluing is
  geometrically/equivariantly admissible. This requires identifying the slice
  representation and constructing $W_F$ with exactly that equivariant boundary.

- **G3 (Global equivariant assembly preserves $\pi_1$).** Prove $\pi_1(M)\cong\Gamma$ after
  excision of cone neighborhoods and insertion of the $W_F$. Requires van Kampen control:
  the links/spheres and the inserted pieces must be simply connected (or have controlled
  $\pi_1$) so no new generators/relations appear and existing finite-order elements are
  retained with correct order.

- **G4 ($\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity of the assembled universal cover).** Prove
  $H_*(\widetilde M;\mathbb{Q})=H_*(\mathrm{pt};\mathbb{Q})$ via Mayer–Vietoris over the
  $\Gamma$-orbit decomposition, using $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity of each inserted piece and
  the fact that the deleted links are $\mathbb{Q}$-homology spheres. The interplay between
  the contractibility of $X$ and the inserted rationally-acyclic pieces must be made
  rigorous (a spectral-sequence / equivariant homology argument).

- **G5 (Closed manifold without boundary).** Prove the resulting $M$ is a closed manifold:
  the boundaries created in Stage B exactly cancel the excised link boundaries, dimensions
  match, and the smooth (or topological) structure extends across all gluing seams.

- **G6 (Existence of $2$-torsion uniform lattices — well-posedness).** Confirm the
  hypothesis is non-vacuous: exhibit/cite uniform lattices in real semisimple groups
  containing $2$-torsion (e.g. via arithmetic constructions / cocompact lattices with
  finite-order elements), so the constructed answer addresses a genuine class.

---

## Difficulty Assessment

- **G1/q7-4 (Hard).** This is the crux. One must produce an honest *manifold* (not just a
  complex) with a *free* finite action and $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic quotient and *prescribed
  equivariant boundary*. $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic-but-not-acyclic manifolds with free
  $\mathbb{Z}/2$-actions exist, but pinning the boundary to the slice sphere and proving
  rational acyclicity rel boundary needs surgery/handle arguments. The
  Euler-characteristic obstruction shows the naive (closed) object is *impossible*, so the
  construction must be cleverly framed; getting this framing wrong sinks the whole proof.

- **G2 (Moderate–Hard).** Slice representations of finite subgroups of semisimple Lie
  groups are explicit, but realizing exactly that equivariant sphere as $\partial W_F$
  constrains the construction; equivariant smoothing/normal data must be tracked.

- **G3 (Moderate).** Standard van Kampen, but care needed: torsion elements of $\Gamma$
  must survive with correct order; connectivity of inserted pieces must be high enough.

- **G4 (Moderate–Hard).** Equivariant Mayer–Vietoris is standard in principle but the
  bookkeeping over infinitely many orbits (cocompact, so finitely many orbit types but the
  cover is noncompact) and ensuring exact cancellation to $H_*(\mathrm{pt})$ is delicate.

- **G5 (Moderate).** Dimension/parity matching and smooth structure extension; routine if
  G1–G2 are set up correctly, but a genuine obligation.

- **G6 (Easy–Moderate).** Citable from arithmetic group theory, but must be stated.

---

## Suggested Approaches

1. **Orbifold-to-manifold replacement (recommended).** Treat $X/\Gamma$ as an orbifold;
   systematically replace each singular stratum's cone by the manifold piece $V_F=W_F/F$.
   This is the cleanest route and matches the in-progress solver direction (q7-4).

2. **Building block via "rationally invisible" free actions.** Construct $W_F$ for
   $F=\mathbb{Z}/2$ as a (boundary of a) twisted disk bundle or by surgery on
   $S(V)\times[0,1]$ to kill rational homology in the quotient while keeping the action
   free; verify $H_*(W_F/F;\mathbb{Q})$ rel boundary is that of a point.

3. **Transfer first, then realize.** Use the rational transfer to argue the homotopy-level
   $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclicity is automatic for free quotients, reducing the problem to a
   *manifold-realization* (surgery) problem: realize the desired $\mathbb{Q}$-homology
   Poincaré data by an actual manifold with the right $\pi_1$ and boundary.

4. **Smith-theory bookkeeping as a sanity check.** At every stage, confirm the mod-$2$
   homology is *allowed* to be nonzero (Smith theory is not violated because we never claim
   mod-$2$ acyclicity), and confirm the Euler-characteristic constraint is respected by
   keeping boundaries.

5. **Strategy if attempting a NO answer instead.** If one suspected the answer were No, one
   would need an obstruction surviving rationalization (e.g. an integral/$L^2$ or
   $\rho$-invariant / assembly-map obstruction). Given the Euler-characteristic remark and
   the existence of $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic free $\mathbb{Z}/2$-models, this route is unlikely
   to succeed for the full question; the constructive Yes is the intended solution.

---

## References

- Skill file: `skills/math-research/SKILL.md` (proof commandments, gap taxonomy).
- Problem statement: q7 (Weinberger), "Uniform Lattices with 2-Torsion as Fundamental
  Groups."
- Existing tracker issue **q7-4** (in_progress): "Local model: finite 2-torsion group acts
  freely & cocompactly on a finite-dim $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic manifold," including the
  solver-agent's Euler-characteristic correction (closed free $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic model is
  impossible; use $W_F$ with boundary and $\mathbb{Q}$-acyclic *quotient*).
- Theorems cited: Smith theory (free $\mathbb{Z}/p$ actions on $\mathbb{F}_p$-acyclic
  finite-dimensional spaces are impossible); transfer/averaging for finite group
  cohomology over $\mathbb{Q}$; Euler-characteristic multiplicativity for free actions
  ($\chi(W)=|F|\chi(W/F)$); Poincaré duality and surgery realization of Poincaré complexes
  (Wall); van Kampen; equivariant Mayer–Vietoris.
